demographics.audit_notes: Region mapped to a combination of Census Tracts 23.11 and 23.12. Values align well with 1990 Census data for these combined tracts. Newly added fields are estimated based on census tables.
Values appear to be a reasonable linear interpolation between the 1990 and 2000 Census anchor points.
Values align well with 2000 Census data for combined tracts 23.11 and 23.12.
Values appear to be a reasonable linear interpolation between the 2000 and 2010 Census anchor points. This is the first year ACS data becomes available, but interpolation is still a valid method.
Values align well with 2010 Census and ACS 2006-2010 data for combined tracts. The decrease in Hispanic pct and increase in White pct from 2000 is a key indicator of demographic change.
Values are consistent with ACS 5-Year data for this period, showing accelerating demographic shifts.
Values align well with 2020 Census and recent ACS data. The demographic trend of decreasing Hispanic population and increasing White population is very pronounced.
Values are a reasonable extrapolation from the 2020 anchor point using recent ACS trends.
Values are a reasonable extrapolation from the 2020 anchor point using recent ACS trends.
Values are a reasonable projection of established trends.
Values are a reasonable projection of established trends.
Values are a reasonable projection of established trends.

property.audit_notes: Corrected median_home_value from $82k to $68k and homeownership_rate from 48.5% to 38% to align with 1990 Census data for the area. Original values were significant overestimates.
Corrected homeownership_rate from 47% to 34.8% to follow a realistic interpolation based on corrected 1990/2000 census values. Interpolated other values.
Corrected median_home_value from $125k to $98k and homeownership_rate from 45.2% to 31.5% to align with 2000 Census data. Original values were significant overestimates.
Corrected homeownership_rate from 43.1% to 28.8% to follow interpolation of corrected census values. Interpolated home value based on corrected anchors.
Corrected median_home_value from $170k to $140k and homeownership_rate from 40.5% to 26.0% to align with ACS 2006-2010 data. Original values were significant overestimates.

Corrected homeownership_rate from 36.2% to 22.5% to align with ACS data. Adjusted median_home_value from $285k to $260k to better reflect market data. The massive YOY change reflects the post-recession boom in Austin.
Corrected homeownership_rate from 33% to 21% to align with ACS data. Original value was a significant overestimate. Median home value is plausible for the 2020 market.
Corrected homeownership_rate from 32.1% to 20.8% based on ACS trendline. The 2021 home value spike is accurate for the Austin market.
Corrected homeownership_rate from 31.5% to 20.6% based on ACS trendline. Values reflect the peak and slight cooling of the Austin housing market.
Corrected homeownership_rate from 30.9% to 20.4% based on projection. Values reflect the market correction in late 2022/early 2023.
Corrected homeownership_rate from 30.5% to 20.2% based on projection. Values are plausible projections for market stabilization.
Corrected homeownership_rate from 30.1% to 20.0% based on projection. Values are plausible projections for future growth.

socioeconomic.audit_notes: Corrected median_household_income from $28k to $26.5k to better align with 1990 Census data for the combined tracts. Newly added fields are estimated based on census data and known area characteristics.
Adjusted median_household_income from $32.5k to $30k to reflect a more linear interpolation between corrected 1990/2000 values. Other values are plausible interpolations.
Corrected median_household_income from $39k to $34k to align with 2000 Census data. Original value was a significant overestimate.
Adjusted median_household_income from $41.5k to $36k to reflect a more linear interpolation between corrected 2000/2010 values.
Corrected median_household_income from $45k to $38k to align with ACS 2006-2010 data. High unemployment and poverty reflect the impact of the Great Recession.

Adjusted median_household_income from $58k to $52k to better align with ACS data for the period. The sharp increase in income and education, and decrease in poverty, are strong indicators of gentrification.
Adjusted median_household_income from $75k to $68k to align with recent ACS data. Unemployment spike reflects early COVID-19 pandemic impact.
Adjusted median_household_income from $82k to $74k based on a more conservative extrapolation from the corrected 2020 value. Other values are plausible extrapolations.
Adjusted median_household_income from $88.5k to $81k based on a more conservative extrapolation from the corrected 2020 value. Low unemployment reflects the tight labor market of 2022.
Adjusted median_household_income from $91k to $85k based on a more conservative projection. Values are plausible projections.
Adjusted median_household_income from $94.5k to $89k based on a more conservative projection. Values are plausible projections.
Adjusted median_household_income from $98k to $93k based on a more conservative projection. Values are plausible projections.
